2605.07937.title: Ask Early, Ask Late, Ask Right: When Does Clarification Timing Matter for Long-Horizon Agents?
2605.07937.paper_summary: Long-horizon AI agents execute complex workflows spanning hundreds of sequential actions, yet a single wrong assumption early on can cascade into irreversible errors. When instructions are incomplete, the agent must decide not only whether to ask for clarification but when, and no prior work measures how clarification value changes over the course of execution. We introduce a forced-injection framework that provides ground-truth clarifications at controlled points in the agent's trajectory across four information dimensions (goal, input, constraint, context), three agent benchmarks, and four frontier models (three per benchmark; one on a single benchmark only; 84 task variants; 6,000+ runs). Counter to the common intuition that "earlier is always better," we find that the value of clarification depends sharply on what information is missing: goal clarification loses nearly all value after 10% of execution (pass@3 drops from 0.78 to baseline), while input clarification retains value through roughly 50%. Deferring any clarification type past mid-trajectory degrades performance below never asking at all. Cross-model Kendall tau correlations (0.78-0.87 among models sharing identical task coverage; 0.34-0.67 across the full 4-model panel) confirm these timing profiles are substantially task-intrinsic. A complementary study of 300 unscripted sessions reveals that no current frontier model asks within the empirically optimal window, with strategies ranging from over-asking (52% of sessions) to never asking at all. These empirical demand curves provide the quantitative foundation that existing theoretical frameworks require but have lacked, and establish concrete design targets for timing-aware clarification policies. Code and data will be publicly released.
2605.07937.paper_summary_zh: 長期的 AI 代理執行跨越數百個順序動作的複雜工作流程，但早期的一個錯誤假設可能會導致不可逆的錯誤。當指令不完整時，代理必須決定不僅是是否要求澄清，還有何時要求，而之前的工作並未衡量澄清的價值在執行過程中如何變化。我們引入了一個強制注入框架，在代理的軌跡中的控制點提供真實的澄清，涵蓋四個信息維度（目標、輸入、約束、上下文）、三個代理基準和四個前沿模型（每個基準三個；僅一個在單一基準上；84 個任務變體；超過 6,000 次運行）。與「越早越好」的常見直覺相反，我們發現澄清的價值在於缺失的信息：目標澄清在執行 10% 之後幾乎失去所有價值（pass@3 從 0.78 降至基線），而輸入澄清的價值大約保持到 50%。將任何澄清類型延遲到中途後，性能會降至低於完全不詢問的水平。跨模型的 Kendall tau 相關性（在共享相同任務覆蓋的模型之間為 0.78-0.87；在整個四模型面板中為 0.34-0.67）確認這些時間配置在實質上是任務固有的。一項對 300 次非腳本會議的補充研究顯示，當前的前沿模型都未在經驗最佳窗口內提出要求，策略範圍從過度詢問（52% 的會議）到完全不詢問。這些經驗需求曲線提供了現有理論框架所需但缺乏的定量基礎，並為時間感知的澄清政策建立了具體的設計目標。代碼和數據將公開發布。
2605.07937.author: Anmol Gulati et.al.
2605.07937.authors: Anmol Gulati, Hariom Gupta, Elias Lumer, Sahil Sen, Vamse Kumar Subbiah
